summary: Regression test after fix for infinite navigation loop (blank/flashing screen). Verified all 3 seeded users (no-role, teacher, student) plus unauthenticated across 5 entry paths each = 20 navigation scenarios. All scenarios stabilised in 1-6 navigations (previous bug reported ~1074 in 4s). Also verified Configurações renders 'Conectar Google Classroom' button for the no-role user, sidebar navigation works cleanly for teacher (Turmas, Alunos, Configurações) and student (Minhas Atividades, Configurações), and Login page renders for unauthenticated users. All 28 existing backend pytest cases still pass (test_classroom_oauth.py + test_role_and_ai.py).

issues: Cosmetic only — Portuguese '(a)' gets capitalized to '(A)' by the 'capitalize' Tailwind class in the H2 'Bem-vindo(a) de volta, professor(a).' rendering as 'Bem-vindo(A) de volta, professor(A).'. Same class issue was reported in iteration_4 on Configurações. Suggest replacing the 'capitalize' class on both places or moving the label to already-cased plain text.

action_items: OPTIONAL/COSMETIC (carried over from iteration_4): drop the 'capitalize' Tailwind class on the H2 in /app/frontend/src/pages/Login.jsx (line 82-84) and the Papel span in Configuracoes.jsx so '(a)' stops rendering as '(A)'. Fix does not affect functionality.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js — the fix is correct: RoleHome and RequireRole now redirect a role-less authenticated user to /configuracoes (which is accessible to all authenticated users regardless of role) instead of '/'. Because '/' would render Login which in turn navigates to /home when user is present, the previous circuit /→/home→/dashboard→/ was infinite. New target /configuracoes has no navigate-away useEffect so the loop terminates.
App.js — the fallback route '<Route path="*" element={<Navigate to="/home" replace />}/>' is safe because /home is protected and routes through RoleHome which now always terminates.

Login.jsx useEffect still navigates to /home when user is present — this is fine now because RoleHome handles the no-role case. However, if a future change removes /configuracoes from the ProtectedRoute list without also updating RoleHome/RequireRole, the loop would reappear. Consider a small comment above the useEffect and above RoleHome/RequireRole cross-referencing each other to prevent regression.
Recommend adding a client-side loop-guard in AppRouter (e.g. useRef counter reset on any 500ms window) as defence-in-depth for future misconfigurations.
server.py still 843 lines (unchanged from iter-4) — split into modules recommended but not blocking.
auth.jsx: refresh() sets loading=false in finally — correct. No changes needed.

success_rate.backend: 100% (28/28 pytest cases, includes a real Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.5 call)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (20/20 navigation scenarios <10 hops; 5/5 sidebar clicks navigated in 1 hop; connect-classroom-button visible for no-role user; Login page renders for unauth)

test_credentials: TEACHER: session_token=seed_teacher_session, user_id=seed_teacher_1, role=teacher. STUDENT: session_token=seed_student_session, user_id=seed_student_1, role=student. NO-ROLE: session_token=seed_test_session_educrm_1, user_id=test_professor_1. All in test_database.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data — reused iteration_4 seeds (users, user_sessions, classroom_tokens). Fixture ensure_seed in test_role_and_ai.py continues to be idempotent.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Navigation-loop scenario is fully regressed via a Playwright script that (a) sets the session_token cookie for each of the three seeded users, (b) hooks page.on('framenavigated'), (c) visits 5 entry URLs per scenario, and (d) asserts each stabilises in <10 hops. Run backend regression with: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. For the frontend, reuse the block in this iteration's mcp_browser_automation output; the assertions to preserve are: teacher / → /dashboard, student / → /minhas-atividades, no_role /* → /configuracoes, unauth /* → /, and the connect-classroom-button must be visible for the no-role user on /configuracoes.
rca of the issue: Fixed. Root cause was two-place misdirection: (1) RoleHome fell through to /dashboard when user.role was null/undefined, and (2) RequireRole('teacher') then redirected to '/' (Login) instead of a role-tolerant page, and (3) Login.jsx's useEffect immediately navigated authenticated users back to /home — closing the loop. The fix in App.js adds an explicit 'if (!user.role) return <Navigate to="/configuracoes" replace/>' in both RoleHome and RequireRole, and changes the wrong-role branch of RequireRole to go to /home (which resolves through RoleHome and terminates) instead of '/'. Verified empirically: with seed_test_session_educrm_1 (no classroom_tokens), all entry paths converge to /configuracoes in exactly 4 hops (1 hop for direct /configuracoes visit). No path exceeded 6 hops for any user.
